channel.title: Iheb Gafsi
channel.description: Notes on geometric deep learning, representation learning, and applied research.

item.title: Why gimbal lock can never be fixed, and why that's the same fact as a bad map of the world
item.description: A short, checkable proof that no three-number coordinate system for 3D rotations can ever avoid a singularity, why compactness is the entire reason, where Euler angles and rotation vectors each hide theirs, the hairy ball theorem as a sibling fact, and why this is the exact same theorem that makes every map of the Earth distorted somewhere.

item.title: SO(3) and SE(3): the groups underneath every rotation
item.description: What makes SO(3) a group, the tangent space and exponential map, exactly why Euler angles hit gimbal lock at pitch = 90 degrees, the SU(2) double cover, and how SE(3) extends all of it to rigid motion.

item.title: A short history of learning where things go: hand-crafted features to geometry-aware embeddings
item.description: From SIFT and bag-of-words, through word2vec and distributional semantics, to contrastive and masked self-supervision, to choosing embedding geometry (hyperbolic, equivariant) on purpose. Same question throughout: what should the representation space look like.

item.title: Beyond the grid: a short tour of geometric deep learning
item.description: Convolutions assume a grid. Graphs, point clouds, and meshes don't have one. A walkthrough of equivariance, permutation-invariant message passing, receptive field growth on real vs small-world topology, and where rotation equivariance connects back to the orientation-fusion project.

item.title: Demo post: math, diagrams, and plots in markdown
item.description: A template post showing every supported block: KaTeX math, a Mermaid diagram, a Plotly chart, and a code block. Delete once you've read data/posts.json and README.md.
